name: Jeff Dean
bio: Chief Scientist, Google DeepMind & Google Research. Gemini Lead. Opinions stated here are my own, not those of Google. TensorFlow, MapReduce, Bigtable, ...

tweets.text:
⚡ Excited to announce Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite! We’ve set a new standard for efficiency and capability to give developers our fastest, most cost-effective Gemini 3 model yet.

We engineered this model with thinking levels, allowing it to handle high-volume queries instantly, while scaling up its reasoning for complex edge cases.

By the numbers:
⏱️ 2.5X faster time-to-first-token than 2.5 Flash while being significantly higher quality
📉 $0.25 per 1M input tokens
📊 1432 Elo on LMArena & 86.9% on GPQA Diamond

Thrilled to see what developers build with this kind of speed and quality at scale. Available now in Google AI Studio and Vertex AI.

https://t.co/Weal73Juh8

Here's a side-by-side speed and accuracy comparison comparing Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite against our older Gemini 2.5 Flash model.  It is not only significantly faster in terms of tokens/s, it also needs many fewer tokens to accomplish complex tasks (~1/3 as many in this case). https://t.co/g0pPKULQRW

Here's a side-by-side speed and accuracy comparison comparing Gemini 3.1 Flash Lite against our older Gemini 2.5 Flash model.  Not only is the new model significantly faster in terms of tokens/s, it also needs many fewer tokens to accomplish complex tasks (~1/3 as many in this case).

RT @prajdabre: Btw this question is a homage to @JeffDean and @GhemawatSanjay, who wrote the paper on MapReduce.
